title.en: Pole bean cultivation — up-country intermediate + wet zones

summary.en: HORDI recommendation for pole bean (Phaseolus vulgaris L.) in up-country intermediate and wet zones — Badulla, Nuwara Eliya, Matale, Kandy districts. Soil pH 5.5–6.5; flowering temperature below 30 °C. Spacing 50 × 40 cm, 50 kg seed/ha. Two-stage fertilizer schedule. First harvest 60+ days after planting. Yield: standard bush 5–10 t/ha; Gannoruwa BIL 20 t/ha; Kekulu Bonchi 25 t/ha.

field_provenance.notes: Yield range spans standard bush bean (5–10 t/ha ≈ 2,000–4,000 kg/acre) to the high-yield Kekulu Bonchi pole bean (25 t/ha ≈ 10,000 kg/acre).

body.en: Soil pH 5.5–6.5. Target flowering-stage temperature under 30 °C — Badulla, Nuwara Eliya, Matale, Kandy areas are ideal.
TSP and MOP rates shown are 'after soil test' — adjust downward on soils testing high for P or K.
Direct seed at 50 cm row × 40 cm within-row. Pole bean seed rate 50 kg/ha.

name.en: Pole bean seed (Balangoda Nil / Bandarawela Green / Gannoruwa BIL / Kekulu Bonchi / Gannoruwa Green / Kappetipola Nil / HORDI Green / Kentucky Wonder Green / Lanka Butter / HORDI Bean-03)

body.en: Pole beans start producing 60+ days after planting; bush types 45+ days. Pick every 2–3 days at marketable pod length.

notes.en: Bean rust and anthracnose risk rises under cool wet up-country conditions. Rotate fungicides per DOA 2019 pesticide recommendation; avoid overhead irrigation.
Bean fly (Ophiomyia phaseoli) bores in young stems. Protect seedlings with a registered systemic insecticide within the first 3 weeks.
